message:
feat(fcp-sandbox): expand sandboxing with egress filtering and WASI support

Sandbox isolation and network egress control:

egress.rs (+416 lines):
- Domain allowlist: glob patterns, subdomain matching, punycode handling
- IP range blocking: RFC 1918 private ranges, link-local, multicast
- Port restrictions: allowlist, denylist, well-known port mapping
- Protocol filtering: TCP/UDP/ICMP controls, DNS resolution interception
- Egress audit logging: connection attempts, blocked requests, quota usage

linux.rs (+168 lines):
- Seccomp BPF profiles: syscall filtering, argument inspection
- Namespace isolation: user, network, mount, PID namespace creation
- Cgroup v2 limits: memory, CPU, I/O bandwidth constraints
- Capability dropping: preserve only required POSIX capabilities
- Landlock filesystem sandboxing: read-only mounts, path restrictions

sandbox.rs (+196 lines):
- Sandbox lifecycle: create, configure, enter, monitor, destroy
- Resource limits: file descriptor count, process count, address space
- Environment sanitization: PATH reduction, secret variable filtering
- Temp directory isolation: per-sandbox /tmp with quota enforcement
- Sandbox escape detection: suspicious syscall patterns, kernel exploit heuristics

wasi.rs (+244 lines):
- WASI preview1 compatibility: fd_read, fd_write, path_open stubs
- WASI preview2 resources: streams, pollables, filesystem types
- Clock abstraction: monotonic, wall-clock with drift compensation
- Random source: CSPRNG passthrough with rate limiting
- Environment/argument passing: UTF-8 validation, size limits
- Preopened directories: capability-based filesystem access

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
